metadata.diversity_inclusion_rule: when one top1 ontology family covers at least 60% of the active queue, append all rows from non-dominant families so expert review does not collapse to one chemistry
metadata.provenance_rule: imports append review provenance and preserve existing evidence.sources
metadata.unlabeled_inclusion_rule: append all unlabeled queue rows even when ranked below the export cutoff

current_label.rationale: Quinoprotein glucose dehydrogenase uses PQQ-dependent glucose oxidation outside the current flavin, heme, cobalamin, and hydrolase seed fingerprints.

mechanism_text_snippets: Crystallographic evidence [PMID:10508152] strongly supports the direct hydride transfer from the glucose to the C5 of the PQQ cofactor. Followed by re-oxidation of the PQQ cofactor by ubiquinone.

The addition-elimination reaction comprises general base catalysed proton abstraction, followed by the formation of a covalent PQQ-substrate adduct, and oxidative elimination of the product. Followed by re-oxidation of the PQQ cofactor by ubiquinone.
Alternative mechanism in which a direct hydride transfer from the glucose to the C4 of the PQQ cofactor occurs. Followed by re-oxidation of the PQQ cofactor by ubiquinone.
